results.title: Recce: Explore, validate, and share data impact before merging | Product Hunt
results.content:
We started with the Recce Open Source toolkit to help data practitioners inspect and validate changes locally.  
  
Now, with Recce Cloud, teams can collaborate on and share validation results as part of building a reliable data review process that scales.  
  
Validate your work as a data engineer, and share data checks with stakeholders and reviewers.  
  
The goal is to turn every data change into a clear, confident, and collaborative review, and enable teams can move faster without breaking trust.  
  
We’re just getting started, and we’d love your feedback.  
  
Check it out, and let us know what you think!  
  
CL Kao  
  
Founder, Recce [...] Hey Product Hunt! I’m CL, founder of Recce.  
   
After two decades building developer tools, including one of the first distributed version control systems used by Apple, I've witnessed firsthand how transforming the development process fundamentally reshapes entire industries.  
  
As data-centric development workflows are taking over, we’ve hit a breaking point and the way we build and review data changes hasn’t kept up. This becomes the bottleneck for turning company's own data as their most valuable asset.  
  
- Software engineers have CI and structured code reviews.  
- Data teams too, but the automated parts are often shallow amd syntatic. mostly we rely on manual checks, scattered context, and fire-fighting to catch and fix issues.  
  
We built Recce to fix that. [...] Subscribe

results.title: TalktoData AI Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
4.4

Based on 7 reviews

Review TalktoData AI?

TalktoData AI is praised for its user-friendly approach to data analysis, allowing users to connect data sources and ask questions in plain language without coding. Users appreciate its real-time insights and visualizations, making it accessible to both beginners and experts. The tool is noted for its enterprise-ready capabilities, seamless integration, and customization options, which enhance organizational data-driven decision-making. Some users express a desire for more advanced features like predictive analytics. Overall, TalktoData is highly recommended for its simplicity and effectiveness in democratizing data analysis.

# The best data visualization tools in 2026

Data visualization tools turn raw data into clear charts, maps, and dashboards. They help teams explore trends, track behavior, and share insights across products.

Grafana
Metabase
Hex
IONI
Basedash: AI data analyst
nivo
Framer 3.0
Promoted

## Top reviewed data visualization tools [...] ## Frequently asked questions about Data visualization tools

Real answers from real users, pulled straight from launch discussions, forums, and reviews.

### Q: Which data visualization tools offer AI-assisted chart creation and natural language querying?

Basedash and Hex are the clearest picks for AI-assisted charting and natural-language querying.

Basedash: AI-native Autopilot creates charts from plain-English prompts, handles complex or ambiguous questions, and connects across 600+ sources so the agent can reason across datasets.

Hex: AI agents speed dashboard creation and let you upload CSVs/spreadsheets — every cell becomes queryable and usable by the AI features. [...] Basedash: AI data analyst

We ve been growing really fast (30%+ MoM ARR) at @Basedash since launching last year. Most of that growth has been the result of hard work, but we ve also had a secret weapon: an AI agent that acts as both a data analyst and a PM, working 24/7 to optimize our product s activation and conversion rates.

For decades, companies have been making product decisions based on intuition and manual data analysis. We wanted to see what would happen if AI could take the wheel completely.

#### What's great [...] ### Top forum threads

Rohit

#### What's great

What I liked most about Hex is that it makes complex data analysis feel much more accessible and interactive. The agentic workflow layer is pretty interesting because it allows users to explore data, generate insights, and build visualizations without manually handling every technical step themselves

#### What needs improvement

One thing that could probably improve is making the generated analytical workflows a little more transparent for users. Sometimes with agentic systems, having better visibility into how insights or calculations are being generated can help users trust the outputs more confidently

#### vs Alternatives

Gemini 2.5 Pro
Claude Code auto-fix [...] #### vs Alternatives

Gemini 2.5 Pro
Claude Code auto-fix 

After exploring analytics-focused AI products, I feel different models are better at different parts of the workflow. Claude feels stronger for structured reasoning and long analytical tasks, GPT-4o feels faster and more flexible for interactive workflows, while Gemini is pretty useful for research-heavy and data-connected tasks. For a platform like Hex, combining strong reasoning with fast data interaction is probably the most important part

Riia Jarvenpaa
